453→        private fun setupMenu() {
454→            // Enable menu
455→            (requireActivity() as MenuHost).addMenuProvider(
456→                object : MenuProvider {
457→                    override fun onCreateMenu(menu: Menu, menuInflater: MenuInflater) {
458→                        menu.clear()
459→                        menuInflater.inflate(R.menu.card_template_editor, menu)
460→
461→                        if (templateEditor.tempModel!!.notetype.isCloze) {
462→                            Timber.d("Editing cloze model, disabling add/delete card template and deck override functionality")
463→                            menu.findItem(R.id.action_add).isVisible = false
464→                            menu.findItem(R.id.action_add_deck_override).isVisible = false
465→                        } else {
466→                            val template = getCurrentTemplate()
467→
468→                            @StringRes val overrideStringRes = if (template != null && template.has("did") && !template.isNull("did")) {
469→                                R.string.card_template_editor_deck_override_on
470→                            } else {
471→                                R.string.card_template_editor_deck_override_off
472→                            }
473→                            menu.findItem(R.id.action_add_deck_override).setTitle(overrideStringRes)
474→                        }
475→
476→                        // It is invalid to delete if there is only one card template, remove the option from UI
477→                        if (templateEditor.tempModel!!.templateCount < 2) {
478→                            menu.findItem(R.id.action_delete).isVisible = false
479→                        }
480→
481→                        // marked insert field menu item invisible for style view
482→                        val isInsertFieldItemVisible = currentEditorViewId != R.id.styling_edit
483→                        menu.findItem(R.id.action_insert_field).isVisible = isInsertFieldItemVisible
484→                    }
485→
486→                    override fun onMenuItemSelected(menuItem: MenuItem): Boolean {
487→                        val col = templateEditor.getColUnsafe
488→                        val tempModel = templateEditor.tempModel
489→                        when (menuItem.itemId) {
490→                            R.id.action_add -> {
491→                                Timber.i("CardTemplateEditor:: Add template button pressed")
492→                                // Show confirmation dialog
493→                                val ordinal = templateEditor.viewPager.currentItem
494→                                // isOrdinalPendingAdd method will check if there are any new card types added or not,
495→                                // if TempModel has new card type then numAffectedCards will be 0 by default.
496→                                val numAffectedCards = if (!CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(tempModel!!, ordinal)) {
497→                                    col.notetypes.tmplUseCount(tempModel.notetype, ordinal)
498→                                } else {
499→                                    0
500→                                }
501→                                confirmAddCards(tempModel.notetype, numAffectedCards)
502→                                return true
503→                            }
504→                            R.id.action_insert_field -> showInsertFieldDialog()
505→                            R.id.action_delete -> {
506→                                Timber.i("CardTemplateEditor:: Delete template button pressed")
507→                                val res = resources
508→                                val ordinal = templateEditor.viewPager.currentItem
509→                                val template = tempModel!!.getTemplate(ordinal)
510→                                // Don't do anything if only one template
511→                                if (tempModel.templateCount < 2) {
512→                                    templateEditor.showSimpleMessageDialog(res.getString(R.string.card_template_editor_cant_delete))
513→                                    return true
514→                                }
515→
516→                                if (deletionWouldOrphanNote(col, tempModel, ordinal)) {
517→                                    return true
518→                                }
519→
520→                                // Show confirmation dialog
521→                                val numAffectedCards = if (!CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(tempModel, ordinal)) {
522→                                    Timber.d("Ordinal is not a pending add, so we'll get the current card count for confirmation")
523→                                    col.notetypes.tmplUseCount(tempModel.notetype, ordinal)
524→                                } else {
525→                                    0
526→                                }
527→                                confirmDeleteCards(template, tempModel.notetype, numAffectedCards)
528→                                return true
529→                            }
530→                            R.id.action_add_deck_override -> {
531→                                displayDeckOverrideDialog(tempModel!!)
532→                                return true
533→                            }
534→                            R.id.action_preview -> {
535→                                performPreview()
536→                                return true
537→                            }
538→                            R.id.action_confirm -> {
539→                                Timber.i("CardTemplateEditor:: Save model button pressed")
540→                                if (modelHasChanged()) {
541→                                    val confirmButton = templateEditor.findViewById<View>(R.id.action_confirm)
542→                                    if (confirmButton != null) {
543→                                        if (!confirmButton.isEnabled) {
544→                                            Timber.d("CardTemplateEditor::discarding extra click after button disabled")
545→                                            return true
546→                                        }
547→                                        confirmButton.isEnabled = false
548→                                    }
549→                                    launchCatchingTask(resources.getString(R.string.card_template_editor_save_error)) {
550→                                        requireActivity().withProgress(resources.getString(R.string.saving_model)) {
551→                                            withCol { tempModel!!.saveToDatabase() }
552→                                        }
553→                                        onModelSaved()
554→                                    }
555→                                } else {
556→                                    Timber.d("CardTemplateEditor:: model has not changed, exiting")
557→                                    templateEditor.finish()
558→                                }
559→
560→                                return true
561→                            }
562→                            R.id.action_card_browser_appearance -> {
563→                                Timber.i("CardTemplateEditor::Card Browser Template button pressed")
564→                                val currentTemplate = getCurrentTemplate()
565→                                currentTemplate?.let { launchCardBrowserAppearance(it) }
566→                                return true
567→                            }
568→                        }
569→                        return false
570→                    }
571→                },
572→                viewLifecycleOwner,
573→                Lifecycle.State.RESUMED
574→            )
575→        }

587→        fun performPreview() {
588→            val col = templateEditor.getColUnsafe
589→            val tempModel = templateEditor.tempModel
590→            Timber.i("CardTemplateEditor:: Preview on tab %s", templateEditor.viewPager.currentItem)
591→            // Create intent for the previewer and add some arguments
592→            val i = Intent(templateEditor, CardTemplatePreviewer::class.java)
593→            val ordinal = templateEditor.viewPager.currentItem
594→            val noteId = requireArguments().getLong("noteId")
595→            i.putExtra("ordinal", ordinal)
596→            i.putExtra("cardListIndex", 0)
597→
598→            // If we have a card for this position, send it, otherwise an empty card list signals to show a blank
599→            if (noteId != -1L) {
600→                val cids = col.getNote(noteId).cardIds(col)
601→                if (ordinal < cids.size) {
602→                    i.putExtra("cardList", longArrayOf(cids[ordinal]))
603→                }
604→            }
605→            // Save the model and pass the filename if updated
606→            tempModel!!.editedModelFileName =
607→                CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(templateEditor, tempModel.notetype)
608→            i.putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModel.editedModelFileName)
609→            onRequestPreviewResult.launch(i)
610→        }

665→        private fun deletionWouldOrphanNote(col: Collection, tempModel: CardTemplateNotetype?, position: Int): Boolean {
666→            // For existing templates, make sure we won't leave orphaned notes if we delete the template
667→            //
668→            // Note: we are in-memory, so the database is unaware of previous but unsaved deletes.
669→            // If we were deleting a template we just added, we don't care. If not, then for every
670→            // template delete queued up, we check the database to see if this delete in combo with any other
671→            // pending deletes could orphan cards
672→            if (!CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(tempModel!!, position)) {
673→                val currentDeletes = tempModel.getDeleteDbOrds(position)
674→                // TODO - this is a SQL query on GUI thread - should see a DeckTask conversion ideally
675→                if (col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(tempModel.modelId, currentDeletes) == null) {
676→                    // It is possible but unlikely that a user has an in-memory template addition that would
677→                    // generate cards making the deletion safe, but we don't handle that. All users who do
678→                    // not already have cards generated making it safe will see this error message:
679→                    templateEditor.showSimpleMessageDialog(resources.getString(R.string.card_template_editor_would_delete_note))
680→                    return true
681→                }
682→            }
683→            return false
684→        }

721→        /**
722→         * Confirm if the user wants to delete all the cards associated with current template
723→         *
724→         * @param tmpl template to remove
725→         * @param notetype model to remove template from, modified in place by reference
726→         * @param numAffectedCards number of cards which will be affected
727→         */
728→        private fun confirmDeleteCards(tmpl: JSONObject, notetype: NotetypeJson, numAffectedCards: Int) {
729→            val d = ConfirmationDialog()
730→            val msg = String.format(
731→                resources.getQuantityString(
732→                    R.plurals.card_template_editor_confirm_delete,
733→                    numAffectedCards
734→                ),
735→                numAffectedCards,
736→                tmpl.optString("name")
737→            )
738→            d.setArgs(msg)
739→
740→            val deleteCard = Runnable { deleteTemplate(tmpl, notetype) }
741→            val confirm = Runnable { executeWithSyncCheck(deleteCard) }
742→            d.setConfirm(confirm)
743→            templateEditor.showDialogFragment(d)
744→        }
745→
746→        /**
747→         * Confirm if the user wants to add new card template
748→         * @param notetype model to add new template and modified in place by reference
749→         * @param numAffectedCards number of cards which will be affected
750→         */
751→        private fun confirmAddCards(notetype: NotetypeJson, numAffectedCards: Int) {
752→            val d = ConfirmationDialog()
753→            val msg = String.format(
754→                resources.getQuantityString(
755→                    R.plurals.card_template_editor_confirm_add,
756→                    numAffectedCards
757→                ),
758→                numAffectedCards
759→            )
760→            d.setArgs(msg)
761→
762→            val addCard = Runnable { addNewTemplate(notetype) }
763→            val confirm = Runnable { executeWithSyncCheck(addCard) }
764→            d.setConfirm(confirm)
765→            templateEditor.showDialogFragment(d)
766→        }

768→        /**
769→         * Execute an action on the schema, asking the user to confirm that a full sync is ok
770→         * @param schemaChangingAction The action to execute (adding / removing card)
771→         */
772→        private fun executeWithSyncCheck(schemaChangingAction: Runnable) {
773→            try {
774→                templateEditor.getColUnsafe.modSchema()
775→                schemaChangingAction.run()
776→            } catch (e: ConfirmModSchemaException) {
777→                e.log()
778→                val d = ConfirmationDialog()
779→                d.setArgs(resources.getString(R.string.full_sync_confirmation))
780→                val confirm = Runnable {
781→                    templateEditor.getColUnsafe.modSchemaNoCheck()
782→                    schemaChangingAction.run()
783→                    templateEditor.dismissAllDialogFragments()
784→                }
785→                val cancel = Runnable { templateEditor.dismissAllDialogFragments() }
786→                d.setConfirm(confirm)
787→                d.setCancel(cancel)
788→                templateEditor.showDialogFragment(d)
789→            }
790→        }

792→        /**
793→         * @param tmpl template to remove
794→         * @param notetype model to remove from, updated in place by reference
795→         */
796→        private fun deleteTemplate(tmpl: JSONObject, notetype: NotetypeJson) {
797→            val oldTemplates = notetype.getJSONArray("tmpls")
798→            val newTemplates = JSONArray()
799→            for (possibleMatch in oldTemplates.jsonObjectIterable()) {
800→                if (possibleMatch.getInt("ord") != tmpl.getInt("ord")) {
801→                    newTemplates.put(possibleMatch)
802→                } else {
803→                    Timber.d("deleteTemplate() found match - removing template with ord %s", possibleMatch.getInt("ord"))
804→                    templateEditor.tempModel!!.removeTemplate(possibleMatch.getInt("ord"))
805→                }
806→            }
807→            notetype.put("tmpls", newTemplates)
808→            Notetypes._updateTemplOrds(notetype)
809→            // Make sure the fragments reinitialize, otherwise the reused ordinal causes staleness
810→            (templateEditor.viewPager.adapter as TemplatePagerAdapter).ordinalShift()
811→            templateEditor.viewPager.adapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
812→            templateEditor.viewPager.setCurrentItem(newTemplates.length() - 1, templateEditor.animationDisabled())
813→        }
814→
815→        /**
816→         * Add new template to a given model
817→         * @param model model to add new template to
818→         */
819→        private fun addNewTemplate(model: JSONObject) {
820→            // Build new template
821→            val oldCardIndex = requireArguments().getInt(CARD_INDEX)
822→            val templates = model.getJSONArray("tmpls")
823→            val oldTemplate = templates.getJSONObject(oldCardIndex)
824→            val newTemplate = Notetypes.newTemplate(newCardName(templates))
825→            // Set up question & answer formats
826→            newTemplate.put("qfmt", oldTemplate.getString("qfmt"))
827→            newTemplate.put("afmt", oldTemplate.getString("afmt"))
828→            // Reverse the front and back if only one template
829→            if (templates.length() == 1) {
830→                flipQA(newTemplate)
831→            }
832→            val lastExistingOrd = templates.getJSONObject(templates.length() - 1).getInt("ord")
833→            Timber.d("addNewTemplate() lastExistingOrd was %s", lastExistingOrd)
834→            newTemplate.put("ord", lastExistingOrd + 1)
835→            templates.put(newTemplate)
836→            templateEditor.tempModel!!.addNewTemplate(newTemplate)
837→            templateEditor.viewPager.adapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
838→            templateEditor.viewPager.setCurrentItem(templates.length() - 1, templateEditor.animationDisabled())
839→        }
840→
841→        /**
842→         * Flip the question and answer side of the template
843→         * @param template template to flip
844→         */
845→        @KotlinCleanup("Use Kotlin's Regex methods")
846→        private fun flipQA(template: JSONObject) {
847→            val qfmt = template.getString("qfmt")
848→            val afmt = template.getString("afmt")
849→            val m = Pattern.compile("(?s)(.+)<hr id=answer>(.+)").matcher(afmt)
850→            if (!m.find()) {
851→                template.put("qfmt", afmt.replace("{{FrontSide}}", ""))
852→            } else {
853→                template.put("qfmt", m.group(2)!!.trim { it <= ' ' })
854→            }
855→            template.put("afmt", "{{FrontSide}}\n\n<hr id=answer>\n\n$qfmt")
856→        }
857→
858→        /**
859→         * Get name for new template
860→         * @param templates array of templates which is being added to
861→         * @return name for new template
862→         */
863→        private fun newCardName(templates: JSONArray): String {
864→            // Start by trying to set the name to "Card n" where n is the new num of templates
865→            var n = templates.length() + 1
866→            // If the starting point for name already exists, iteratively increase n until we find a unique name
867→            while (true) {
868→                // Get new name
869→                val name = resources.getString(R.string.card_n_name, n)
870→                // Cycle through all templates checking if new name exists
871→                if (templates.jsonObjectIterable().all { name != it.getString("name") }) {
872→                    return name
873→                }
874→                n += 1
875→            }
876→        }

object DiscardChangesDialog {
    fun showDialog(
        context: Context,
        positiveButtonText: String,
        negativeButtonText: String,
        message: String,
        positiveMethod: () -> Unit
    ) = AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
        message(text = message)
        positiveButton(text = positiveButtonText) { positiveMethod() }
        negativeButton(text = negativeButtonText)
    }
}
"}

Launching skill: firebender-help

---
name: firebender-help
description: Official Firebender documentation and configuration guide. CRITICAL - Firebender is a DIFFERENT coding agent than Cursor or Claude Code. Use this skill whenever the user asks about Firebender configuration, features, commands, or functionality. If the user is trying to create a skill, agents.md, rules, sub agent, configure MCP, and more, you should likely consider using this skill because the context will likely be relevant for what the user wants in order to execute it.
version: 0.15.4
---

# Firebender Help & Documentation

## About Firebender

Firebender is the best coding agent for Android engineers and JetBrains IDEs. It's purpose-built for Android Studio and IntelliJ IDEA with deep integrations for Jetpack Compose, Gradle, and mobile development workflows. It works great for all languages and all jetbrains IDEs too.

## CRITICAL: Firebender is NOT Cursor or Claude Code

**DO NOT make assumptions about Firebender configuration based on other tools.**

Firebender has its own unique:
- Configuration system (firebender.json)
- Command syntax
- Rules system
- MCP integration
- Feature set
- Jetbrains LSP tools (go-to-definition, find usages, renaming symbols, etc.)
- Android-specific tooling

## Mandatory Workflow for Helping Users

When helping with Firebender questions, you MUST:

### Step 1: Read the Documentation First
Use the Read tool to access the relevant doc file from this skill's `docs/` directory.

### Step 2: Cite Your Sources
Quote the EXACT lines from the documentation that answer the user's question.
Show the precise configuration syntax or command usage from the docs.

### Step 3: Implement or Answer
Only after citing documentation sources, proceed with:
- Creating configuration files
- Running commands
- Answering questions
- Making changes

## Available Documentation

The documentation is organized in the `docs/` subdirectory. Use the `docs.json` file to understand the complete navigation structure and find relevant pages.

## How to Access Documentation

The documentation files are extracted to `~/.firebender/.cache/skills/firebender-help-0.15.4/docs/` on first use.

To read a doc file:
1. Identify which doc is relevant by reading `<skill-base-path>/docs/docs.json`
2. Use the Read tool with the path: `<skill-base-path>/docs/<path-to-file>`

The base directory path is shown when this skill is invoked - look for "Base directory for this skill:" in the tool result.

**Note:** Built-in Firebender skills are cached in `~/.firebender/.cache/skills/`, while user-defined skills go in `~/.firebender/skills/`.

## Linking to Online Documentation

When citing documentation, provide web URLs so users can reference them online.

**URL Pattern:** `https://docs.firebender.com/<page-path>`

The `<page-path>` comes from the "pages" entries in `docs.json`. To construct URLs:
1. Read `<skill-base-path>/docs/docs.json` to see the navigation structure
2. Find the page entry that matches the documentation file (without .mdx/.md extension)
3. Construct the URL as `https://docs.firebender.com/<page-entry>`

## Version Lock

This documentation matches Firebender plugin version **0.15.4**.
User's plugin version may differ - configurations could vary between versions.

## Key Principle

**Sources first, implementation second.**
Never guess Firebender's behavior - always verify with bundled documentation first.

package com.ichi2.anki.dialogs

import android.content.Context
import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
import com.ichi2.utils.message
import com.ichi2.utils.negativeButton
import com.ichi2.utils.positiveButton
import com.ichi2.utils.show

object DiscardChangesDialog {
    fun showDialog(
        context: Context,
        positiveButtonText: String,
        negativeButtonText: String,
        message: String,
        positiveMethod: () -> Unit
    ) = AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
        message(text = message)
        positiveButton(text = positiveButtonText) { positiveMethod() }
        negativeButton(text = negativeButtonText)
    }
}
